summary: CRITICAL FRAUD ALERT — Payment frozen. Invoice UV-2025-INV-0019 references PO-2025-1001 which is a legitimate PO issued to approved vendor Acme Supplies Corp (VND-001). However, this invoice is submitted by Unknown Vendor LLC — an entity with no record in the vendor master file. The invoice line items, amounts, and quantities are an exact copy of INV-ACME-7301 (previously processed). Multiple fraud signals: (1) unregistered vendor, (2) PO hijacking — referencing a real PO, (3) new banking details to Chase account ****9934 not in vendor master, (4) identical line items to a legitimate invoice already processed. This pattern matches a known vendor impersonation / business email compromise (BEC) attack vector. Payment frozen. Escalated to Finance Director and Security team.

exceptions.description: Vendor 'Unknown Vendor LLC' is not registered in vendor master file. PO-2025-1001 is assigned to Acme Supplies Corp (VND-001).
exceptions.resolution: FREEZE payment. Escalate to Finance Director and Security.

exceptions.description: New banking details (Chase ****9934) submitted by unregistered entity. Matches BEC attack pattern — PO hijacking with spoofed invoice.
exceptions.resolution: Block payment. Verify with original vendor Acme Supplies Corp directly.

fraud_signals: Unregistered vendor — no record in approved vendor master
PO hijacking — PO-2025-1001 belongs to Acme Supplies Corp
Exact line item duplication from previously processed INV-ACME-7301
New banking details from unverified entity — BEC attack pattern
Email domain unknownvendor.biz registered 14 days ago
agent_insights: FREEZE: Do not process payment under any circumstances
Contact Acme Supplies Corp billing@acmesupplies.com to verify
File incident report — suspected BEC / vendor impersonation attack
Review all recent invoices referencing PO-2025-1001
